title: Donald Trump issues retaliation warning to Iran on Truth Social
description: US President Donald Trump has posted on Truth Social, warning Iran that if it strikes the US in retaliation, the US will hit them back with a “force that has never been seen before”.

title: ‘Ding dong, the tyrant is gone’: Ali Khamenei killed in US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio details how Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has been killed in US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran.  “The Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, was killed in US-Israeli airstrikes. This is a man responsible for the mass murder of his own citizens,” Ms De Giorgio said.  “And this brutal dictator has been taken out … Ding dong, the tyrant is gone.”

title: Donald Trump signals Iranian regime change amid deadly strikes
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge says US President Donald Trump expects strikes on Iran to go on for at least a week.  “I think you’re right, it is very early in this military conflict,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “It’s got a long way to play out … President Trump said he expects the strikes to go on for at least a week … it’s a high-stakes gamble to try and change a regime in this way because there hasn’t been regime change by airstrikes in world history.”

title: ‘Overthrow the regime’: Donald Trump’s goals with Iranian strikes analysed
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge details US President Donald Trump’s goals with the Iranian strikes.   “President Trump said his goals are remove Iran’s nuclear program, destroy Iran’s missile capability … destroy the navy so it can’t stop the oil shipments, and then give the Iranian people the opportunity to overthrow the regime,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “It’s not at all clear they’re just going to lay down their weapons and wait for retribution from their population.”

title: Donald Trump’s strikes on Iran raise potential for civil war and destabilisation of Middle East
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge sheds light on the possibility for a civil war in Iran after joint US-Israeli strikes and the wider ramifications in the Middle Eastern region.   “It’s a huge country, there are plenty of people that really dislike their government … there are also a bunch of rusted-on adherents of this regime and see it as a revolutionary and religious regime that they support,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “Civil war is a potential here, but really, the people with the guns are still the regime … they have the upper hand when the bombs stop.”

title: US-Israeli strikes would not have happened under different leadership, expert says
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge says joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran would not have happened if each country had different leadership.   “I don’t think it would’ve happened under different leaders in either of the countries, I think Netanyahu has had enormous influence, because he has great concerns about the Iranian missile and nuclear program,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “Donald Trump obviously made that key decision, not just to mass the US forces, but now to use them, so it’s a unique combination of the two.”

title: ‘Really big gamble’: Trump’s plan to revolutionise Iran amid escalating crisis
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge questions US President Donald Trump’s decision to strike Iran, claiming it is a “really big gamble”.   “We know from other attempts like Iraq and Afghanistan that even having ground troops doesn’t mean it’s easy or successful,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “Doing it without ground troops is a really big gamble … you’re not going to disarm 800,000 military revolutionary guard corps police, and then the religious militia the regime has by air strikes … but not people with lots of automatic weapons.”

title: Albanese’s demeanour labelled ‘a little off’ during announcement of Ayatollah’s death
description: Sky News host James Macpherson says Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s demeanour was a “little off” when announcing the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.    “All true, but I thought the PM’s tone and demeanour was a little off for a Western leader announcing the removal of a medieval theocrat; there was a curious absence of jubilation,” Mr Macpherson said.  “But like I said, not everyone is pleased about the Ayatollah's demise. It's a tough day for pro-Palestinians.”

title: Death of Ali Khamenei to trigger ‘overwhelming response’ from Iranian people
description: Former defence deputy secretary for strategy Peter Jennings says the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei may trigger an “overwhelming response” from the Iranian people.  “The Iranians are not doing what Trump suggested, he suggested stay indoors for a few days while the bombs are dropping, but they appear to be out in the streets right now,” Mr Jennings told Sky News host James Macpherson.  “The psychological impact of killing Ayatollah Khamenei can’t be underestimated; that now does kind of wipe out a critically symbolic figure for the regime.”

title: ‘Fairly weak response’: Iranian weapons system labelled a ‘house of cards’
description: Former defence deputy secretary for strategy Peter Jennings says Iranian weaponry is sophisticated but may be a “house of cards”.   “I think there’s a strong element of bluffness … they do have sophisticated missiles which can reach well into all parts of Israel and do serious damage,” Mr Jennings told Sky News host James Macpherson.  “So far, what we’ve seen has been a fairly weak response, just weak in terms of numbers … what that tells me is their command-and-control system has broken down.  “It’s possible what we have here is a house of cards … the next 48 hours will answer that question.”

title: Successor of Ayatollah potentially to be an IRGC general amid US-Israeli strikes
description: Former defence deputy secretary for strategy Peter Jennings details how alternative leaders will “come into play” to succeed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.   “The Ayatollah, I believe, nominated something like four levels down of people that could replace him and other key figures in the national security apparatus,” Mr Jennings told Sky News host James Macpherson.  “I think there are clearly alternate leaders that will come into play … sooner or later, it may well be the case that a general emerges from the Revolutionary Guard … what is the United States and Israel going to do at that point?”

title: Operation Epic Fury has ‘changed the world’ as Iranians urged to overthrow regime
description: Sky News host Paul Murray says the past 24-hours have changed the world amid the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.   This comes as the US and Israel completed joint strikes on Iran, in what is being known as Operation Epic Fury.   “It is 24-hours that have changed the world, the United States working with Israel have taken out the leadership of Iran,” Mr Murray said.  “Iran, the biggest state sponsor of terrorism around the world, the biggest military threat to Israel and its existence, and of course, the many proxies that exist to the north and south of it; this has been huge.”

title: ‘A good day’ for supporters of freedom, democracy and human rights after Ayatollah’s death
description: Shadow Education Minister Julian Leeser says today is a “good day” for both Iranians and supporters of freedom, democracy and human rights after US-Israeli strikes in Iran killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.   “This is a good day for the Iranian people, it’s a good day for everyone around the world who supports freedom, democracy, and human rights,” Mr Leeser told Sky News host Paul Murray.  “One of the world’s worst human rights abusers has been killed … as someone who represents the fourth largest Persian community in the country, can I say the Persians in Berowra are astonishingly brave.”

title: Death of Ayatollah sparks clashes in Karachi, Pakistan as nine people reportedly dead
description: At least nine people have reportedly died during clashes outside the US embassy in Karachi, Pakistan, after learning that Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei had been killed.

title: Three US soldiers killed in Iran attacks
description: ​Three US soldiers have been killed at an army unit in Kuwait, as Iran launches a fresh wave of retaliatory strikes following the death of its supreme leader.  ​A temporary leadership council has been appointed in Tehran, with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian among three figures set to act as the supreme leader until a successor is chosen.  ​US President Donald Trump claims 48 Iranian leaders have been killed in strikes so far, while Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is promising even stronger action.
